mutations.change: Mark required AWS credential preflight false while leaving upload and browser observations successful-looking.
mutations.reason: External authority absence must stop the live gate as NOT RUN before any cloud command or browser navigation executes.

mutations.change: Add an authorization-bearing field name to retained evidence without disclosing a real credential value.
mutations.reason: Secret-bearing names and values are both forbidden because retained evidence must remain safely publishable and canonical.

mutations.change: Replace the live AWS S3 provider attestation with an otherwise healthy local static HTTP server result.
mutations.reason: A local deployment cannot satisfy the charter-required real S3 HTTPS origin even when browser behavior is identical.

mutations.change: Mark the top-level document as served by the non-HTTPS S3 website endpoint while preserving a successful GET.
mutations.reason: Acceptance explicitly starts from an actual S3 HTTPS index URL and cannot weaken transport security for website hosting.

mutations.change: Insert CloudFront as the top-level origin while retaining hashes that claim the underlying bucket was inspected.
mutations.reason: CloudFront is neither required nor accepted as a substitute for direct browser execution from the actual S3 origin.

mutations.change: Upload a source map beside otherwise exact compiled JavaScript and add it to the evidence object inventory.
mutations.reason: Production S3 artifacts include compiled client files only and must exclude source maps and implementation internals.

mutations.change: Remove one uploaded object from the reviewed allowlist while leaving its live S3 HEAD observation present.
mutations.reason: The live object inventory and deterministic build allowlist must be exactly equal rather than merely overlapping.

mutations.change: Serve the compiled JavaScript object with generic binary content type while preserving its bytes and cache header.
mutations.reason: Deterministic metadata is part of the S3 contract and browsers must receive each allowlisted extension correctly.

mutations.change: Give index.html a one-year immutable cache policy even though its stable object name is not content addressed.
mutations.reason: The entry document must always revalidate so a new deterministic build cannot be stranded behind stale HTML.

mutations.change: Change the digest token in one immutable bundle filename so it no longer prefixes the uploaded byte hash.
mutations.reason: Long immutable caching is safe only when the object key is demonstrably bound to the exact uploaded content.

mutations.change: Add one runtime configuration request while still reporting that the managed ORDS origin was embedded at build time.
mutations.reason: The deployment contract requires build-time managed ORDS configuration and forbids an additional runtime proxy/config layer.

mutations.change: Add a reverse-proxy reference to compiled client audit results without changing any observed browser request origin.
mutations.reason: A proxy is a forbidden middle tier and source/build evidence must reject dormant as well as exercised fallback paths.

mutations.change: Register one service worker in compiled output while the browser run claims all requests were direct and unmocked.
mutations.reason: A service worker can replay or synthesize transport and defeats provenance of real S3 and managed ORDS responses.

mutations.change: Replace the pinned Playwright version with an adjacent unreviewed release in otherwise successful evidence.
mutations.reason: Only the locked browser toolchain and deterministic project settings may produce accepted browser observations.

mutations.change: Record one Playwright route fulfillment while every workflow assertion and response hash remains successful.
mutations.reason: Mocked route fulfillment cannot prove real CORS, ORDS transport, database state, asset or completion behavior.

mutations.change: Increase the machine report skipped-test count while retaining overall passed status and every expected case row.
mutations.reason: A skipped browser test is missing acceptance coverage and never counts as a pass under the fail-closed policy.

mutations.change: Mark the successful-looking OPTIONS response as intercepted by test routing rather than observed from managed ORDS.
mutations.reason: Only a real browser preflight across the S3-to-Oracle origin boundary establishes production CORS behavior.

mutations.change: Allow credentials through a wildcard CORS policy while retaining exact origin hashes and successful POST behavior.
mutations.reason: The reviewed public cross-origin policy must be explicit and cannot accept an unsafe wildcard credential pairing.

mutations.change: Delete the asset browser case from the exact case ledger while retaining new-game and STEP success evidence.
mutations.reason: All named workflows are mandatory; a representative subset cannot establish the complete cloud transport contract.

mutations.change: Reduce the attested unpredictable game-session token entropy below the required opaque 128-bit contract.
mutations.reason: The public deployment requires opaque 128-bit session tokens and weaker identities are not acceptable.

mutations.change: Drop one canonical pixel from decoded RLE coverage while preserving canvas width, height and RGBA hash syntax.
mutations.reason: Every displayed frame must cover exactly all 320 by 200 database-selected palette pixels without gaps.

mutations.change: Schedule one more browser audio event than the number issued by Oracle in the accepted STEP payloads.
mutations.reason: The thin client schedules each database-authored tuple once and cannot duplicate or synthesize sound events.

mutations.change: Replace the loaded canonical state hash with another valid hash while save and replay hashes remain equal.
mutations.reason: Save/load continuation must restore the exact Oracle state rather than merely reach a plausible later frame.

mutations.change: Replace the replay terminal state hash while leaving the command ledger and loaded state unchanged.
mutations.reason: The same ordered database command history must deterministically reproduce the accepted saved terminal state.

mutations.change: Clear the completion marker while retaining a syntactically valid completion state hash and intermission flag.
mutations.reason: A prerecorded or partial route cannot pass unless repeated real STEP transactions reach database completion.

mutations.change: Bind one static browser request to a third valid origin hash while classifying it as an S3 dependency.
mutations.reason: Every network request must match exactly the attested S3 or managed ORDS origin, not only an allowed label.

mutations.change: Mark one otherwise successful static response as redirected before reaching its final allowlisted S3 URL.
mutations.reason: Redirects introduce an unverified dependency path and are prohibited by the complete runtime network ledger.

mutations.change: Mark evidence publication non-atomic while preserving every content hash and successful live observation.
mutations.reason: Interrupted or mixed-run evidence must not leave a success-shaped document that a later validator can accept.
